LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS FREEMAN DECORATING SERVICES, INC., CHAMPION EXPOSITION SERVICES, LLC AND WCP EXPOSITIONS OPERATING COMPANY, LLC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. CROSS-COMPLAINTS MAY BE BROUGHT AGAINST ANY PERSON FROM WHOM DEFENDANT SEEKS EQUITABLE INDEMNITY, AND NEED ONLY ALLEGE THAT THE HARM FOR WHICH DEFENDANT IS BEING SUED IS ATTRIBUTABLE, AT LEAST IN PART, TO THE CROSS-DEFENDANT. AMERICAN MOTORCYCLE ASS'N V. SUP. CT (1978) 20 CAL.3D 578, 607. THE INTERACTION OF THE PARTIAL INDEMNITY DOCTRINE WITH THE CROSS-COMPLAINT PROCEDURES WORKS NO UNDUE PREJUDICE TO THE RIGHTS OF PLAINTIFFS. ID. AT 606. MOREOVER, LEAVE TO FILE A PERMISSIVE CROSS-COMPLAINT MAY BE GRANTED "IN THE INTEREST OF JUSTICE" AT ANY TIME DURING THE COURSE OF THE ACTION. CCP 428.50(C). DEFENDANT CLAIMS THAT, DUE TO NEWLY LEARNED FACTS, THE POTENTIAL CROSS-DEFENDANTS MAY BE LIABLE TO DEFENDANT FOR INDEMNITY AND/OR CONTRIBUTION. MOREOVER, DEFENDANT PROVIDED A REASONABLE EXCUSE FOR THE DELAY IN ACQUIRING THESE FACTS-I.E., THE POTENTIAL PREJUDICE DEFENDANT WOULD HAVE FACED HAD THE DEPOSITION OF PALMISANO OCCURRED PRIOR TO THE END OF 2013. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF PALMISANO DELIVERY SERVICE OF SAN FRANCISCO, LLC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. FOR ISSUE NOS. 1 - 3, PLAINTIFF MOVES FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF SEPARATE ELEMENTS OF THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT THAT DOES NOT COMPLETELY DISPOSE OF THE CAUSE ACTION. (CCP 437C(F)(1).) THERE IS NO INDICATION THAT PLAINTIFF HAS COMPLIED WITH CCP 437C(S) TO ADJUDICATE PART OF A CAUSE OF ACTION. FOR ISSUE NO. 4, PLAINTIFF MOVES FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE ANSWER, BUT FAILS TO IDENTIFY ANY SPECIFIC AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE IN ITS NOTICE OF MOTION. (CRC 3.1350(B).) NOTWITHSTANDING THIS PROCEDURAL ISSUE, FREEMAN RESPONDED THAT IT DID NOT CONTRIBUTE DIRECTLY OR PROXIMATELY TO THE INCIDENT. (PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE, EXHIBIT J., P. 12:1-2.) THUS, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PROOF TO SHOW THAT THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES ARE UNSUPPORTED BY FACTUALLY DEVOID DISCOVERY RESPONSES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SARA LERSCHEN CSR6213.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS WHEELHOUSE SOLUTIONS, LLC, FREEMAN DECORATING SERVICES, INC AND CHAMPION EXPOSITION SERVICES, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THE RELIEF SOUGHT IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION DOES NOT PERTAIN TO A SPECIFIC DUTY. (CRC 3.1350(B).) "FREEMAN SEEKS SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS A MATTER OF LAW WITH REGARD TO PDS'S CLAIM THAT FREEMAN HAS OR HAD A CONTRACTUAL DUTY TO PROVIDE PDS WITH BUSINESS PURSUANT TO THE SEPTEMBER 2010 EXCLUSIVE SERVICES AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE PLAINTIFF AND [CHAMPION]. FREEMAN SEEKS AN ORDER HOLDING THAT FREEMAN HAD NO INDEPENDENT OBLIGATION TO PDS PURSUANT TO THE AGREEMENT." (NOTICE OF MOTION, P. 2:1-5.) THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO THE MEANING OF "INDEPENDENT". ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SARA LERSCHEN CSR6213.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-08-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Whitney Davis, Esq., 916.333.5363, for plaintiff Palmisano Delivery Service of San Francisco, LLC.
Marc Tefalu, Esq., 415.438.4600, for defendants Wheelhouse Solutions, LLC, Freeman Decorating Services Inc. and Champion Exposition Services, LLC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF PALMISANO DELIVERY SERVICE OF SAN FRANCISCO, LLC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied. For Issue nos. 1 - 3, Plaintiff moves for summary adjudication of separate elements of the first cause of action for breach of contract that does not completely dispose of the cause action. (CCP 437c(f)(1).) There is no indication that Plaintiff has complied with CCP 437c(s) to adjudicate part of a cause of action. For Issue no. 4, Plaintiff moves for summary adjudication of the Answer, but fails to identify any specific affirmative defense in its Notice of Motion. (CRC 3.1350(b).) Notwithstanding this procedural issue, Freeman responded that it did not contribute directly or proximately to the incident. (Plaintiff's Evidence, Exhibit J., p. 12:1-2.) Thus, Plaintiff has not met its initial burden of proof to show that the affirmative defenses are unsupported by factually devoid discovery responses. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sara Lerschen CSR6213.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Ntc
Off Calendar Apr-08-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Whitney Davis, Esq., 916.333.5363, for plaintiff Palmisano Delivery Service of San Francisco, LLC.
Marc Tefalu, Esq., 415.438.4600, for defendants Wheelhouse Solutions, LLC, Freeman Decorating Services Inc. and Champion Exposition Services, LLC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs WHEELHOUSE SOLUTIONS, LLC, FREEMAN DECORATING SERVICES, INC and CHAMPION EXPOSITION SERVICES, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied. The relief sought in the Notice of Motion does not pertain to a specific duty. (CRC 3.1350(b).) "Freeman seeks summary adjudication as a matter of law with regard to PDS's claim that Freeman has or had a contractual duty to provide PDS with business pursuant to the September 2010 Exclusive Services Agreement between the plaintiff and [Champion]. Freeman seeks an order holding that Freeman had no independent obligation to PDS pursuant to the Agreement." (Notice of Motion, p. 2:1-5.) There is a triable issue of fact as to the meaning of "independent". Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sara Lerschen CSR6213.

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File X-Complt
Off Calendar Apr-04-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Marc T. Cefalu, Esq. of Cox Wootton Lerner Griffin Hansen & Poulos LLP, Tel. 415.438.4600, for defendants and moving party;
Whitney A. Davis, Esq. of The Davis Law Firm, Tel. 916.333.5363, for plaintiff and opposing party, by Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS FREEMAN DECORATING SERVICES, INC., CHAMPION EXPOSITION SERVICES, LLC and WCP EXPOSITIONS OPERATING COMPANY, LLC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT is granted. Cross-complaints may be brought against any person from whom Defendant seeks equitable indemnity, and need only allege that the harm for which Defendant is being sued is attributable, at least in part, to the cross-defendant. American Motorcycle Ass'n v. Sup. Ct (1978) 20 Cal.3d 578, 607. The interaction of the partial indemnity doctrine with the cross-complaint procedures works no undue prejudice to the rights of plaintiffs. Id. at 606. Moreover, leave to file a permissive cross-complaint may be granted "in the interest of justice" at any time during the course of the action. CCP 428.50(c). Defendant claims that, due to newly learned facts, the potential cross-defendants may be liable to Defendant for indemnity and/or contribution. Moreover, Defendant provided a reasonable excuse for the delay in acquiring these facts-i.e., the potential prejudice Defendant would have faced had the deposition of Palmisano occurred prior to the end of 2013. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
